items.title: Gold Holds Gain After Warsh Remarks Ease Fed Rate-Hike Prospects
items.summary: Gold held a rebound after a speech by US Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh dampened speculation the central bank may hike interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

items.title: Asian Stocks to Decline as Traders Weigh AI, Warsh: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Stocks in Asia were set to fall on Thursday as a selloff in US chipmakers weighed on sentiment and investors assessed fresh remarks from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Ukraine Takes the War to Russia
items.summary: Kyiv’s aim is simple: Put enough pressure on President Vladimir Putin that he agrees to end the war. Will it work?

items.title: Oil Extends Decline as Barrels Flow Through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil fell for a third day as flows through the Strait of Hormuz surged and there were signs of progress in indirect talks between the US and Iran.

items.title: REalloys Announces Partnership to Operate on US Army Base
items.summary:
REalloys CEO Lipi Sternheim said that members of the US military could be actively involved in working with his company to mine rare earth minerals after the announcement of a deal to let REalloy mine on US Army bases. 
Sternheim said that the partnership with the Army shows how 'strategically important' rare earth metals are to the United States.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: Iran war live: ‘Positive progress’ as US, Tehran wrap up indirect talks
items.summary: Tehran says a 'communication channel' will be established with Washington to report and discuss breaches of the MoU.

items.title: How North Korean POWs caught Seoul in a Ukraine arms ‘conundrum’
items.summary:
Ukraine’s unresolved decision regarding two North Korean prisoners of war who have said they want to go to South Korea has given Kyiv leverage as it presses Seoul to sell weapons for its war against Russia, observers say.
The soldiers, captured in early 2025 after being deployed to Kursk to support Russia’s war effort, are considered South Korean nationals under Seoul’s constitution, which defines

items.title: Trump sees progress as US and Iran hold talks in Qatar
items.summary:
US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that indirect talks with Iran in Qatar were making progress, offering a tentative sign that diplomacy was holding after recent exchanges of fire threatened efforts to end the Middle East war.
Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi, who led Tehran’s delegation, later said the talks had concluded and that the sides had agreed to establish a commu

items.title: U.S. and Iran Set to Hold Indirect Talks After Trading Attacks: What to Know
items.summary: Negotiators are in Qatar for indirect talks and ship traffic has risen in the Strait of Hormuz, even as Tehran and Washington remain far apart on key issues. Here’s what to know.
